content:
Given the undirected dynamic graph with the edges:
[(0, 5, 0), (1, 5, 3), (2, 3, 8), (2, 4, 9), (2, 7, 9), (0, 6, 12), (2, 8, 12), (4, 6, 13), (4, 9, 17), (0, 2, 20), (3, 4, 25), (7, 8, 26), (2, 6, 27), (1, 3, 28)],

We need to find the nodes linked with node 0 exactly at time 20.

First, we will scan each edge and collect the other node whenever the time t matches the queried time AND one of the nodes matches the queried node.

* Edge (0, 5, 0) has node 0 and time 0. -> Ignore.
* Edge (1, 5, 3) has node 1 and time 3. -> Add node 5.
* Edge (2, 3, 8) has node 2 and time 8. -> Add node 3.
* Edge (2, 4, 9) has node 2 and time 9. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (2, 7, 9) has node 2 and time 9. -> Add node 7.
* Edge (0, 6, 12) has node 0 and time 12. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (2, 8, 12) has node 2 and time 12. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (4, 6, 13) has node 4 and time 13. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (4, 9, 17) has node 4 and time 17. -> Add node 9.
* Edge (0, 2, 20) has node 0 and time 20. -> Add node 2.
* Edge (3, 4, 25) has node 3 and time 25. -> Add node 4.
* Edge (7, 8, 26) has node 7 and time 26. -> Add node 8.
* Edge (2, 6, 27) has node 2 and time 27. -> Add node 6.
* Edge (1, 3, 28) has node 1 and time 28. -> Add node 3.

Now we have the following nodes linked with node 0 at time 20:
2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9

Answer: [2, 3, 4, 6, 7, 8, 9]
